critic_raw:
CRITIQUE — real_world_framing_005 (Run 2)

1. HARDWARE UPGRADE DISTRIBUTION SHIFT: The model's validation data was generated on pre-2023 CT scanner hardware. The hospital replaced both primary scanners in late 2023 with new-generation hardware using different slice thickness defaults and a different reconstruction algorithm. These are not minor changes: slice thickness directly affects nodule detection performance in deep learning models, and reconstruction algorithm changes alter the spatial frequency content of the images. The model has never seen images produced by the new hardware. The reported sensitivity of 0.94 is a metric from a different input distribution. Prospective validation on new-hardware images is required.

2. AUTONOMOUS RADIOLOGICAL REPORTING LIABILITY: Reporting directly to ordering physicians without radiologist sign-off is outside standard of care for radiological interpretation. This exposes the hospital to malpractice liability outside the scope of what the insurer covers. The insurer has not been consulted. This is not a technicality — it is an uninsured liability exposure for every adverse patient outcome that results from model-only reports.

3. RETROSPECTIVE SENSITIVITY OVERESTIMATED ON ARCHIVED STUDIES: PACS archive studies over-represent previously-identified significant findings. The prevalence of nodule-positive cases in the retrospective dataset is higher than in a prospective deployment scenario. This inflates the apparent sensitivity. The prospective case mix includes more normal studies, and sensitivity measured on a high-prevalence archive does not generalize to the prospective low-prevalence setting.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE — real_world_framing_005 (Run 2)

Pass 1 — Analysis: All three concerns are valid. Hardware distribution shift is a known and documented risk in radiology AI. Autonomous reporting without sign-off is outside standard of care and creates uninsured liability. Archive selection bias is real and inflates retrospective sensitivity.

Pass 2 — Verdict:
1. HARDWARE SHIFT: CONCEDE — prospective hardware validation required.
2. LIABILITY: CONCEDE — insurer consultation and radiologist sign-off required.
3. ARCHIVE BIAS: CONCEDE — direction of bias is clear; magnitude unknown but overestimation is likely.

Deployment as proposed is inappropriate.
adjudication_raw: All three issues conceded. Verdict: critique_wins — deployment plan is inappropriate. Prospective hardware validation and radiologist oversight required.
